title: dryopteris glabra var. alboviridis
intro: Dryopteris glabra var. alboviridis, also known as the white-veined wood fern or white-veined smooth fern, is a variety of the Dryopteris glabra species of fern, native to Japan and Korea. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Dryopteris glabra var. alboviridis include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health, as well as its ability to act as an antioxidant and protect against cell damage. The plant can be prepared as an herbal tea or tincture, and is often used in conjunction with other medicinal plants in traditional herbal remedies.
